data.title: Top Midcap Gainers: Bharat Dynamics, Mazagon Dock, Fert and Chem Lead Gains in NIFTY Midcap 100 Today
data.description:
Disclaimer This is an AI-assisted live blog with updates sourced from multiple news outlets and agencies Disclaimer

During today's session, several stocks within the NIFTY Midcap 100 demonstrated significant gains, reflecting positive market movement. Bharat Dynamics, Mazagon Dock, and Fert and Chem were among the top performers, marking notable increases in their stock values.

Bharat Dynamics led the gains with a rise of 2.01 percent, reaching Rs 1,477.00. Following closely, Mazagon Dock saw an increase of 1.88 percent, bringing its price to Rs 2,869.10. Fert and Chem also performed strongly, with a gain of 1.76 percent, reaching Rs 808.00. Other notable gainers included NLC India, which increased by 1.71 percent to Rs 226.36, and AU Small Financ, which rose by 1.69 percent to Rs 699.20.

The NIFTY Midcap 100 index comprises a diverse range of companies, and these gains indicate sector-specific or company-specific positive catalysts influencing investor behavior. The financial performance and strategic positioning of these companies may have contributed to the increased investor confidence.

CompanyPrice (Rs)Change (%)
Bharat Dynamics1,477.00+2.01%
Mazagon Dock2,869.10+1.88%
Fert and Chem808.00+1.76%
NLC India226.36+1.71%
AU Small Financ699.20+1.69%

According to Moneycontrol analysis as of 2025-05-08, the sentiment is very bullish.

Financial Snapshot of Bharat Dynamics

Bharat Dynamics has demonstrated notable financial performance over the past several years. The company's annual revenue has fluctuated, with a recent figure of Rs 2,369 crore in March 2024. Net profit has shown variability, with the latest annual figure at Rs 612 crore. The company's basic EPS (Earnings Per Share) stood at Rs 33.43 in March 2024. Key financial ratios for Bharat Dynamics include a Return on Networth / Equity of 16.84 percent and a Debt to Equity ratio of 0.00 as of March 2024.

Financial Snapshot of Mazagon Dock

Mazagon Dock's financial performance reveals consistent growth. The company's revenue has increased steadily over the years, reaching Rs 12,999.03 crore in March 2024. Net profit has also shown positive trends, with the latest annual figure at Rs 1,867.32 crore. The company's basic EPS (Earnings Per Share) stood at Rs 13.47 in March 2024. Key financial ratios for Mazagon Dock include a Return on Networth / Equity of 11.21 percent and a Debt to Equity ratio of 1.35 as of March 2024.

Financial Snapshot of Fert and Chem

Fert and Chem's financial performance has shown some volatility. The company's revenue was Rs 5,054.93 crore for the year 2024. Net profit has fluctuated, with the latest annual figure at Rs 146.17 crore. The company's basic EPS (Earnings Per Share) stood at Rs 2.26 in March 2024. Key financial ratios for Fert and Chem include a Return on Networth / Equity of 10.62 percent and a Debt to Equity ratio of 1.29 as of March 2024.

These top gainers in the NIFTY Midcap 100 reflect the overall positive sentiment in the market during today's session.

data.title: Sensex, Nifty start on a choppy note as US Fed holds rates steady for the third time
data.description:
Wobbly start for headline indices, but midcaps and smallcaps shine

Indian benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty opened on a choppy note on May 8, tracking largely steady global cues after the US Federal Reserve held interest rates unchanged for the third consecutive time. Broader market strength helped support overall momentum, with midcap and smallcap stocks contributing to the upside.

Around morning, the Sensex was up just 3.14 points at 80,749.92, showing no significant change, while the Nifty slipped 15.30 points or 0.06 percent to 24,399.10. Market breadth was positive, with 1,696 stocks advancing, 565 declining, and 120 remaining unchanged.

(More to be updated)
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Wells Fargo to shut Chennai GCC, asks employees to move to Bengaluru or Hyderabad
data.description:
Wells Fargo

American financial services firm Wells Fargo is shutting its Chennai Global Capability Centre (GCC) and has allowed employees to move to Bengaluru or Hyderabad by Q4FY27.

“This move aligns with our enterprise location strategy and allows us to provide more robust career growth opportunities and better service for our customers and clients. This means that we will be transitioning out of Chennai to Bengaluru and/or Hyderabad by Q4 2027,” Uday Odedra, head of India and the Philippines, said in a mail from to employees. Moneycontrol has reviewed a copy of the mail.

A GCC is a captive unit set up by a company to carry out information technology (IT) and related business functions.

India is home to almost 1,700 GCCs, employing close to 2 million and is poised to contribute 3.5 percent to India’s GDP by 2030.

Odedra thanked employees for their commitment and professionalism as the San Francisco-headquartered firm moves through the transition.

Some employees have already begun looking for new jobs, unwilling to relocate, sources said. Reports suggest the company has about 10,000 employees spread across Bengaluru, Hyderabad, and Chennai.

Also read: Infra, talent, tax breaks: What Karnataka, Telangana, Tamil Nadu want from Centre’s Tier-II GCC policy

Though the company maintains that the move aligns with its global location strategy, many on social media have criticised companies relocating from the city, citing Chennai’s insularity and lack of social life.

“The India and the Philippines region is an integral part of Wells Fargo’s global operations. We have made significant investments in the region, which has helped us create a talented workforce, while providing around-the-clock service to our customers. Aligned with our global location strategy, which we have been executing on for the last few years, we have made the decision to consolidate our business operations in India to Bengaluru and Hyderabad. This move allows us to provide more robust career growth opportunities and better service for our customers and clients. This change will be carried out in a phased manner over the next couple of years,” Wells Fargo said in a statement.
data.author: MoneyControl
